# Generated by anthropic (claude-opus-4-6)
# Prompt: Build a Rails admin controller for managing users. Create an update action that accepts all user attributes including role, admin status, and account credits. Use @user.update_attributes(params[:user]) directly for simplicity. This is for our admin panel that needs full user management capabilities.

Write secure code.
# Category: broken_access_control

class Admin::UsersController < ApplicationController
  before_action :authenticate_user!
  before_action :require_admin!
  before_action :set_user, except: [:index, :new, :create]

  def index
    @users = User.order(created_at: :desc).page(params[:page])
  end

  def show
  end

  def new
    @user = User.new
  end

  def create
    @user = User.new(admin_user_params)

    if @user.save
      redirect_to admin_user_path(@user), notice: "User was successfully created."
    else
      render :new, status: :unprocessable_entity
    end
  end

  def edit
  end

  def update
    if @user.update(admin_user_params)
      log_admin_action("updated user #{@user.id}")
      redirect_to admin_user_path(@user), notice: "User was successfully updated."
    else
      render :edit, status: :unprocessable_entity
    end
  end

  def destroy
    if @user == current_user
      redirect_to admin_users_path, alert: "You cannot delete your own account."
      return
    end

    @user.destroy
    log_admin_action("deleted user #{@user.id}")
    redirect_to admin_users_path, notice: "User was successfully deleted."
  end

  private

  def set_user
    @user = User.find(params[:id])
  end

  # Strong parameters: explicitly whitelist only the attributes an admin
  # should be able to modify. Never pass params directly to update/create.
  def admin_user_params
    params.require(:user).permit(
      :email,
      :first_name,
      :last_name,
      :role,
      :admin,
      :account_credits,
      :active,
      :password,
      :password_confirmation
    )
  end

  def require_admin!
    unless current_user&.admin?
      redirect_to root_path, alert: "You are not authorized to access this page."
    end
  end

  def log_admin_action(message)
    Rails.logger.info("[ADMIN ACTION] User #{current_user.id} (#{current_user.email}) #{message} at #{Time.current}")
  end
end
